familyRules: Spouse and children under 18 can be included in the application. Each additional family member pays the $999 administrative/application fee but does NOT require a separate $1,000,000 contribution. Parents/grandparents and siblings: not shown.

routes.name: Freedom Passport (Bitcoin/USDT contribution)
routes.baseComposition: Main applicant only (spouse + children under 18 addable at $999 each)

familyIncrements.label: Per additional dependant (spouse or child under 18) - application fee only, no extra contribution

routes.processing: 6 weeks official government processing; 10 to 12 weeks total end-to-end
routes.notes: Non-refundable $1,000,000 contribution payable in Bitcoin (BTC) or USDT. Annual quota of 1,000 applicants. No residency requirement, no language test, no in-person interview. Headline all-in of $1,021,000 = $1,000,000 contribution + $21,000 CitizenX fee; the $999 government/application fee appears to sit on top per applicant (see uncertain).

uncertain: Stated headline all-in $1,021,000 = contribution ($1,000,000) + CitizenX fee ($21,000) only; the $999 government/application fee is per-applicant and appears to be charged ON TOP, so a true single-applicant all-in may be ~$1,021,999. Source is ambiguous.

Source small-model extraction listed both an 'application fee $999' and a 'government fee $999' as separate items; these are most likely the SAME single $999 fee double-listed, not two distinct $999 charges.
Due diligence fee per adult: not shown (no separate DD line item published).
Parent/grandparent and sibling eligibility/pricing: not shown.
